metadata.description: Village generation configuration — placement, tiers, NPCs, structures
notes: Villages spawn during geographic world generation
Tier is selected based on area danger level (higher danger = larger fortified village)
Wall blocks are breakable but not collectible by the player
NPC templates define the villager types that can spawn

placement._target_count_effects: Roughly matches dungeon density across the 262K chunk world.
placement._min_distance_effects: Minimum chunks between village centers. Low because many small hamlets.
placement._valid_danger_effects: Villages can spawn at any danger level. Tier scales with danger.
placement._excluded_effects: Chunk types that cannot contain villages.

tier_selection_by_danger._comment: Maps danger level (1-6) to which tier pool to draw from. Each entry is a list of [tier_name, weight] pairs.

walls._breakable_effects: If true, players can break village walls (but not collect them).
walls._collectible_effects: If true, broken walls drop materials. False = walls just disappear.
walls._health_effects: HP of each wall tile. Higher = harder to break.

entrance_distribution._comment: Entrances distributed evenly across walls. 4=one per side, 5=extra on south, 3=skip one side.

npc_templates.name: Farmer
dialogue_lines: The soil is good this season.
Have you seen any slimes nearby?

npc_templates.name: Guard
dialogue_lines: Halt! Oh, just a traveler. Carry on.
Keep your eyes open out there.
The roads aren't safe at night.

npc_templates.name: Merchant
dialogue_lines: Looking to trade? ...Not yet, I'm afraid.

npc_templates.name: Scholar
dialogue_lines: Ah, a fellow seeker of knowledge!
I've been studying the local wildlife.
Did you know slimes can split when threatened?
